faqs.q: How long would an EazyBase extension take for a home in Enfield?
faqs.a: Your extension is built in our Blackburn factory in as little as four weeks, while your garden stays untouched. Once it arrives in Enfield, installation typically takes under a week on site. Compare that with a traditional build, where trades, skips and scaffolding can occupy your home for months, and the appeal is obvious — especially on a busy residential street.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission from Enfield Council?
faqs.a: Not always. Permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions and garden rooms, though limits apply to size, height and position — and they differ in conservation areas and on land near the Green Belt. We review your property's situation early in the design stage and will always advise you to confirm the position with Enfield Council before work begins. Where a planning application is needed, we prepare the drawings for you.

faqs.q: My street in Enfield is a tight terrace — can you still deliver and install?
faqs.a: Yes, in most cases. Our modules are engineered with real streets in mind, and we survey your access before anything is manufactured — road width, parking restrictions, overhead cables and where any lifting equipment can safely sit. If a parking suspension or temporary permit is needed, we plan for it in advance, so delivery day runs smoothly and your neighbours are inconvenienced for hours, not weeks.

faqs.q: What do Enfield homeowners typically use a modular extension for?
faqs.a: The most popular choices are open-plan kitchen-diners that bring light into the back of a terrace or semi, home offices for people who split the week between Enfield and central London, and garden rooms and playrooms that give growing families breathing space. Because each extension is designed around your home, the same footprint can serve very different lives.

heroHeading: Modular Home Extensions, Made for Enfield
intro:
Enfield has always offered something rare in London: proper houses with proper gardens, at prices that leave room to actually improve them. Whether you're in a bay-fronted terrace near Palmers Green or a semi with a generous plot further east, the space to extend is usually there — it's the disruption and uncertainty of a traditional build that puts people off.

That's exactly the problem EazyBase was built to solve. We design and factory-build modular home extensions in Enfield homes' exact specifications at our facility in Blackburn — in as little as four weeks — then install on site in under a week. No months of mud, skips and scaffolding. Just a beautifully finished kitchen, dining room, home office, playroom or garden room, delivered with the precision only a factory can achieve.

Most of our Enfield customers invest between £20,000 and £60,000, and because so much of the work happens before we arrive, you know your price, your design and your timeline before a single panel is made.

localAngles.heading: Extending an Edwardian terrace on the Palmers Green side
localAngles.body: The Edwardian terraces around Palmers Green, Winchmore Hill and Bush Hill Park are handsome, solidly built homes — but their kitchens were designed for another century. A single-storey rear extension is the classic answer, opening the back of the house into a light-filled kitchen-diner. Factory building suits these streets particularly well: modules are made to millimetre tolerances, so they meet the existing brickwork cleanly, and the short installation window means your neighbours put up with days of activity rather than months of it. We design each extension around your terrace's proportions, not a standard box.

localAngles.heading: Post-war semis and the space to grow
localAngles.body: Head towards eastern Enfield — around Enfield Highway, Ponders End and the streets between — and the housing stock changes: post-war semis and interwar homes with wider plots and longer gardens. That extra space opens up different possibilities. A garden room for working from home, a playroom that gives the living room back to the adults, or a dining extension that finally makes sense of the garden. Because our extensions arrive largely complete, a generous plot means straightforward access and an even quicker installation — often the simplest projects we do.

localAngles.heading: Planning in Enfield: what to know before you start
localAngles.body: Good news first: permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions and detached garden rooms — always confirm the specifics with Enfield Council before committing. There are local wrinkles worth knowing, though. Parts of the borough sit within conservation areas, where rules are tighter, and homes on the northern fringe — around Crews Hill, Forty Hill and Clay Hill — sit close to Green Belt land, which can affect what's permissible on and around your plot. We factor all of this into the design stage, so you're never surprised mid-project, and we'll prepare drawings if a full application is needed.

localAngles.heading: Why factory-built makes sense in outer London
localAngles.body: Enfield's whole appeal is value: more house for your money than almost anywhere inside the M25. A factory-built extension extends that logic. Building in a controlled environment in Blackburn means no weather delays, no unpredictable trade availability and no costs creeping upwards week by week — the things that make traditional London building work so stressful. Your extension is precision-built while your home stays exactly as it is, then installed in under a week. For a £20,000–£60,000 investment, you get certainty on price, quality and timing that a conventional build simply can't offer.

metaDescription: Premium modular home extensions in Enfield. Factory-built in as little as 4 weeks, installed in under a week — kitchens, home offices and garden rooms.
metaTitle: Modular Home Extensions in Enfield | EazyBase
